business_description.nature_of_business: Hotel and restaurant operations; payroll classifications include Hotel All Other Employees (Class 9052), Hotel Bellhops and Doorpersons (Class 9060), Restaurant NOC (Class 9082), and Clerical Office Employees NOC (Class 8810)

coverages_discussed.notes: Policy Number WC-HCHP-2025-3350; policy period 2025-05-01 to 2026-05-01; rating organization Texas Department of Insurance; Risk ID 308382-9453; Large Risk Rating Plan; Interstate Rating applies; Experience Modification Factor 0.94; total TX payroll 16260471

positives: Favorable experience modification factor of 0.94, reflecting actual losses 6% below industry expected level
All claims fully closed with no open reserves — paid losses equal incurred losses across all three policy years
Zero excess losses reported; entire actual loss volume falls within the primary loss layer
Improving claim frequency trend: 8 claims in 2021-2022, declining to 6 in 2022-2023, and 2 in 2023-2024
Actual primary losses of $92,945 are substantially below expected losses of $361,806
concerns: Sixteen total claims recorded across the three-year experience period
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Policy year 2023-2024: 2 claims, $9,940 total paid and incurred, all primary, zero excess. Policy year 2022-2023: 6 claims, $26,662 total paid and incurred, all primary, zero excess. Policy year 2021-2022: 8 claims, $56,343 total paid and incurred, all primary, zero excess. Combined actual primary losses of $92,945 against expected losses of $361,806 yield a calculated experience modification factor of 0.94.

extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Hotel and restaurant operations in Texas with interstate rating designation. Payroll by classification: Class 9052 (Hotel All Other Employees) $9,207,271; Class 9060 (Hotel Bellhops and Doorpersons) $1,454,400; Class 9082 (Restaurant NOC) $3,678,800; Class 8810 (Clerical Office Employees NOC) $1,920,000. Total reported TX payroll: $16,260,471. Rating parameters: Weighting Factor (W) 0.419, Ballast Value (B) $29,114, Expected Excess Losses (Ee) $422,385.
